zh.Bannockburn, Stirlingshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭斯林史爾班諾可本
zh.Belstead, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可貝爾斯亞德
zh.Horsford, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可霍斯福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edmund de Ufford於大約1310年出生在英國素福爾可瑟斯頓時，他的父親Sir Thomas de Ufford32歲，母親Eve De Clavering17歲。他於1330年與Alice結婚。他去世於1375年10月3日在蘇格蘭斯林史爾享年66歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可明日香。

zh-hans.Bannockburn, Stirlingshire, Scotland: 苏格兰斯林史爾班諾可本
zh-hans.Belstead, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可貝爾斯亞德
zh-hans.Horsford, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可霍斯福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edmund de Ufford于大约1310年出生在英国素福爾可瑟斯頓时，他的父亲Sir Thomas de Ufford32岁，母亲Eve De Clavering17岁。他于1330年与Alice结婚。他去世于1375年10月3日在苏格兰斯林史爾享年66岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可明日香。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ufford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ufford (Northamptonshire), Ufford (Suffolk), or Offord Cluny or Offord Darcy (Huntingdonshire). The Northamptonshire and Suffolk placenames derive from the Old English personal name Uffa + Old English worth ‘enclosure’. For the Huntingdonshire placename see Offord , with which the surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Offord
meanings: English (East Anglia and London): habitational name from Offord Cluny and Offord Darcy in Huntindgonshire. The placename derives from Old English upp ‘up, upper’ + ford ‘ford’.
English: alternatively, from a lost place called Offord near Pennyford in Wootton Wawen, Warwickshire. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Offa + Old English worth ‘enclosure’.

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edmund de Ufford was born about 1310, in Thurston, Suffolk, England, his father, Sir Thomas de Ufford, was 32 and his mother, Eve De Clavering, was 17. He married Alice in 1330. He died on 3 October 1375, in Stirlingshire, Scotland, at the age of 66, and was buried in Langley, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edmund de Ufford est né environ 1310, à Thurston, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Ufford, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Eve De Clavering, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Alice en 1330. Il est décédé le 3 octobre 1375, à Stirlingshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterré à Langley, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edmund de Ufford um 1310 in Thurston, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas de Ufford 32 und seine Mutter Eve De Clavering 17. Er hat 1330 Alice geheiratet. Er starb am 3. Oktober 1375 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Stirlingshire, Scotland und wurde in Langley, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edmund de Ufford è nato il circa 1310 nel Thurston, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Sir Thomas de Ufford, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Eve De Clavering, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Alice nel 1330. Morì il 3 ottobre 1375, nel Stirlingshire, Scotland, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolto a Langley, Norfolk, England.

ja.Stirlingshire, Scotland: スコットランドスティルリングシュアイア
ja.Thurston, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクスウルストン
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edmund de Uffordが約1310年にイギリススフォルクスウルストンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Uffordは32歳，母Eve De Claveringは17歳であった。1330年にAliceと結婚した。彼は1375年10月3日にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアで66歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクラングリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edmund de Ufford(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 수폴크 스르스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Ufford(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Eve De Clavering(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1330년 Alice(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1375년 10월 3일 스컷랜드 스티를링실르에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 랭글리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edmund de Ufford nasceu aproximadamente 1310, em Thurston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Ufford, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Eve De Clavering, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice em 1330. Ele faleceu em 3 de outubro de 1375, em Stirlingshire, Scotland, com 66 anos, e foi sepultado em Langley, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edmund de Ufford родился около 1310 г. в местности турстон, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Ufford было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Eve De Clavering, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice в 1330 г. Он умер 3 октября 1375 г. в местности стирлингшир, Шотландия в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности лангли, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edmund de Ufford nació aproximadamente 1310, en Thurston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de Ufford, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Eve De Clavering, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Alice en 1330. Murió el 3 de octubre de 1375, en Stirlingshire, Scotland, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultado en Langley, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
